title: ADR-007: Node Type Unification and Staleness Vocabulary

sections.heading: Status
sections.content:
**Status:** Superseded by ADR-009

**Date:** 2026-03-15

**Decider:** Dante

**Superseded by:** ADR-009 (Unified Staleness and Node Types) — combines this ADR with ADR-008 into a single change.

sections.heading: Context & Problem Statement
sections.content:
The current data model has three overlapping fields for characterizing what a node is:

- `node_type` — 5 values: `atomic_process`, `composite_process`, `document`, `constraint`, `entity`
- `subtype` — sparse optional field: `file`, `section`, `external_package`, `test`
- `tags` — user-defined, separate table, requires a join

This creates three compounding problems:

**1. Ontology violations.** `document` nodes use `composes` edges to link doc files to their sections. The ontology only permits `composes` edges between `composite_process` and `atomic_process` nodes. Every doc file produces one violation per section — currently 426 warnings on every `cortex build`.

**2. Missing own-content staleness for doc sections.** Doc sections have `node_type=document` and are never treated as first-class nodes with hashable content. Their own prose changing is undetected. The only staleness signal for docs is `CONTENT_STALE` — triggered when a *linked code node* changes, not when the section's own content changes.

**3. Staleness vocabulary is fragmented.** `CODE_STALE`, `DESC_STALE`, `CONTENT_STALE`, and `COVERAGE_STALE` apply to different node types with different semantics. `CODE_STALE` (function body changed) and the old `CONTENT_STALE` (doc section linked code changed) use different names for structurally identical signals. `COVERAGE_STALE` (test's validated code changed) and old `CONTENT_STALE` (doc's linked code changed) are the same signal over different edge types with different names.

sections.heading: The Decision
sections.content:
### Part 1 — Unify node_type to three values

`node_type` is reduced to three structural values that describe containment shape only:

- `composite_process` — a container that composes children
- `atomic_process` — a leaf node with hashable content
- `entity` — a data artifact, never stale

`document` and `constraint` are retired as node_type values.

### Part 2 — subtype becomes the full differentiator

`subtype` is now required (not optional) for `atomic_process` and `composite_process` nodes and carries what the node actually is:

| node_type | subtype | what it is |
|---|---|---|
| `composite_process` | `module` | Python module |
| `composite_process` | `docjson` | DocJSON doc file |
| `composite_process` | `external_package` | External dependency |
| `atomic_process` | `function` | Python function or method |
| `atomic_process` | `docjson` | DocJSON doc section |
| `atomic_process` | `test` | Test function |
| `entity` | — | Data artifact |

Test functions are identified by file location: any function in a file whose path matches a `test_paths` entry in `cortex.toml` gets `subtype=test`. No annotation required.

`tags` remain user-defined only. Scanner-assigned differentiation lives in `subtype`, not tags.

### Part 3 — Doc nodes become first-class atomic/composite nodes

DocJSON doc files become `composite_process` + `subtype=docjson`. DocJSON sections become `atomic_process` + `subtype=docjson`. The `composes` edge between them is now ontologically valid — 426 warnings eliminated.

Sections get `code_hash` (prose body hash) and `desc_hash` (section heading hash) stored on index, identical to function nodes. Own-content staleness detection is now active for doc sections.

Composite inheritance via `apply_composite_inheritance()` now propagates staleness up through doc files automatically — no special-casing required.

### Part 4 — Staleness vocabulary: primary and secondary

The staleness vocabulary is reorganized into two tiers:

**Primary staleness** — own content changed, detected via hash comparison, applies uniformly to all `atomic_process` nodes regardless of subtype:

| Signal | Meaning | Detected by |
|---|---|---|
| `CONTENT_STALE` | Body/main content changed | `code_hash` changed, `desc_hash` unchanged |
| `DESC_STALE` | Descriptor/heading changed | `desc_hash` changed, `code_hash` unchanged |

**Secondary staleness** — a dependency changed, detected via graph edges:

| Signal | Meaning | Edge |
|---|---|---|
| `LINKED_STALE` | A node this references changed | `documents` or `validates` |
| `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` | File or node no longer exists | — (existence check) |

`CONTENT_STALE` replaces the old `CODE_STALE`. `LINKED_STALE` replaces both the old `CONTENT_STALE` (doc section whose linked code changed) and `COVERAGE_STALE` (test whose validated code changed) — they are the same signal over different edge types.

**Severity order (for composite inheritance):**
```
STRUCTURAL_DRIFT > CONTENT_STALE > LINKED_STALE > DESC_STALE > CLEAN
```

Primary signals rank above secondary: own-content drift requires editing the node itself. `LINKED_STALE` is softer — the node may still be accurate despite the reference changing.

**Promotion to VERIFIED:**
- `CONTENT_STALE` and `DESC_STALE` are promotable — a human or agent can confirm the node is still accurate.
- `LINKED_STALE` and `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` are not promotable — the cause must be resolved, not attested away.

sections.heading: Consequences
sections.content:
**Positive:**
- 426 ontology violations eliminated. `composes` edges between doc files and sections are now valid.
- Doc sections gain own-content staleness detection — prose changes and heading changes are now tracked.
- Staleness vocabulary is uniform across all node subtypes. `CONTENT_STALE` means body changed everywhere; `DESC_STALE` means descriptor changed everywhere.
- `LINKED_STALE` unifies two formerly separate signals that had identical semantics over different edge types.
- `apply_composite_inheritance()` requires no changes — it will work correctly for doc file → section hierarchies.
- Composite inheritance now propagates doc staleness up through doc files automatically.

**Negative:**
- Migration required: all existing `document` nodes in the DB must be re-typed to `composite_process` / `atomic_process` + `subtype=docjson`. All `constraint` nodes re-typed to `atomic_process` + `subtype=test`.
- All code that branches on `node_type == 'document'` or `node_type == 'constraint'` must be updated to check `subtype` instead.
- The viz color scheme currently colors nodes by `node_type` and must be updated to color by `subtype`.
- ADR-002 is further amended: `CODE_STALE` → `CONTENT_STALE` in the emitted status vocabulary. History table `change_type` values (`CODE_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`) are unchanged.

**Neutral:**
- `entity` nodes are unchanged.
- `external_package` stays in the same position — `composite_process` + `subtype=external_package`.
- `tags` table is unchanged. Scanner no longer writes to it.
